commands.review_summary:
The PR adds secrets/auth provenance evidence to the bundled Policy plugin, including SecretRef/source/insecure-provider and auth-profile metadata checks, docs, and regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection of the current PR head shows blank auth metadata entries are not rejected, then later filtering removes them from the effective requirement set. Current-head real behavior proof for the intended secrets/auth findings is not yet present.
commands.review_followup: This protected, draft, security-sensitive PR needs maintainer stack review and contributor-side current-head proof; automation cannot supply that proof or make the Policy product decision.

target.title: Policy: add secrets auth provenance checks
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a Policy 1.0 secrets/auth provenance category with redacted evidence and doctor/lint findings. Stacked after #81104.

- Records secret provider/input provenance without exposing secret values.
- Enforces `secrets.disallowInline`, `secrets.requireManagedProviders`, `secrets.denySources`, and `secrets.allowInsecureProviders`.
- Adds auth profile metadata/mode checks with `auth.profiles.requireMetadata` and `auth.profiles.allowModes`.
- Documents the new policy rules and JSON evidence shape.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: adds policy conformance evidence and findings for configured secret/auth provenance without exposing raw secret material, including inline secrets, unmanaged SecretRef providers, denied provider sources, insecure secret providers, missing auth-profile metadata, and unapproved auth-profile modes.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu-24.04 source worktrees created from `upstream/main` and PR head `ac8f697003556d3a15f984eb7b7edbf4a076c024`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: created clean detached worktrees for `upstream/main` and `fork/policy-secrets-auth-provenance`; in each worktree ran `node --import ./node_modules/tsx/dist/loader.mjs ./probe-secrets-auth-policy.mjs`. The probe wrote temp `openclaw.jsonc` and `policy.jsonc` files, registered the real policy doctor checks with `registerPolicyDoctorChecks()`, and ran the real doctor/lint path with `runDoctorLintChecks()` against a config containing a sentinel inline secret, an unmanaged env SecretRef, an `exec` secret provider, an insecure file provider, and auth profile policy violations.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

![PR #81974 secrets/auth provenance before/after proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-81974-fresh/pr-81974/pr-81974-secrets-auth-provenance-before-after-proof.png)

```text
Fresh proof artifact: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-81974-fresh/pr-81974/pr-81974-secrets-auth-provenance-before-after-proof.png
Proof summary: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-81974-fresh/pr-81974/summary.txt

eEFORE upstream/main:
status=0
policyRegisterImport=missing-or-failed
importError=ENOENT
checksRegistered=0
findings=0
secretLeaked=false
findingTypes=<none>
ok=false

AFTER PR #81974 head:
status=0
policyRegisterImport=ok
importError=<none>
checksRegistered=6
findings=6
secretLeaked=false
findingTypes=policy/auth-profile-invalid-metadata,policy/auth-profile-unapproved-mode,policy/secrets-denied-provider-source,policy/secrets-inline-value,policy/secrets-insecure-provider,policy/secrets-unmanaged-provider
ok=true
```

- Observed result after fix: `upstream/main` has no secrets/auth policy registration path, so no relevant checks or findings are produced. PR head registers 6 secrets/auth policy checks, reports 6 denied posture findings, and keeps the sentinel inline secret out of serialized findings.
- What was not tested: no live external vault or credential provider was contacted. The proof uses local temp config/policy files and verifies the policy doctor/lint path plus redaction behavior.

## Validation

- `pnpm protocol:check`
- `pnpm check:changed`
